name: Zarich
description: Zarich, also known as Zairich, in Zoroastrian cosmology, is considered a daeva, or demon, embodying the quality of decrepitude and aging. Unlike the Ahuras, who represent the benevolent aspects of existence, Zarich represents the decay and decline inherent in the material world. He is an agent of Angra Mainyu, the destructive spirit, and works to undermine the creations of Ahura Mazda. Zarich's influence is felt in the weakening of the body, the fading of beauty, and the ultimate demise of all living things. He is a constant reminder of the transient nature of earthly existence and the struggle against the forces of corruption. Understanding Zarich allows one to appreciate the Zoroastrian emphasis on resisting decay and upholding the principles of righteousness to combat the forces of evil. The fight against Zarich is ultimately the fight against mortality itself.
subtitle: Demon of Decay

keyMyths.title: Zarich and the First Man
keyMyths.description: When Gayomart, the primordial man, was created by Ahura Mazda, Angra Mainyu unleashed Zarich to hasten his demise. Zarich attacked Gayomart, causing him to weaken and eventually succumb to death after thirty years. From Gayomart's seed sprang the first human couple, but Zarich's influence remained, ensuring their mortality and the continued presence of decay in the world. This myth highlights Zarich's role in introducing mortality to humanity and the ongoing struggle against his influence.

keyMyths.title: The Battle Against Zarich
keyMyths.description: In Zoroastrian eschatology, the final battle against evil involves overcoming the forces of decay embodied by Zarich. Through righteous actions and adherence to the principles of Asha (truth and order), humans can weaken Zarich's influence and contribute to the ultimate triumph of good over evil. This involves maintaining physical and spiritual purity to resist the corrupting forces of decay. This myth emphasizes the importance of individual actions in combating cosmic evil.

keyMyths.title: Zarich and the Plants
keyMyths.description: When plants were first created, Zarich attacked them, causing them to wither and decay. This led to the need for constant care and cultivation to ensure their survival. This myth explains the fragility of life and the constant need to protect and nurture the creations of Ahura Mazda against the forces of decay. It further illustrates the Zoroastrian emphasis on agriculture and maintaining the natural world.
